question: Will the next Prime Minister of Hungary be Viktor Orbán?
narrative_context: Euronews (2026-04-01): TISZA projected 129 seats vs Fidesz 64 — opposition consolidation
PolitPro polling (April 2026): TISZA 49.6% vs Fidesz 39.6% — 10pp gap
21 Research Institute (early April): TISZA 56% vs Fidesz 37% among decided voters
Opposition parties consolidated behind Tisza (others withdrew from race)
Election April 12, 2026 — 10 days from signal creation
evidence: Euronews (2026-04-01): TISZA projected 129 seats vs Fidesz 64, cementing opposition lead
PolitPro Poll Trend: TISZA 49.6%, Fidesz 39.6% (10pp margin)
21 Research Institute: Tisza 56%, Fidesz 37% among decided voters (19pp among decided)
Consolidated opposition: Momentum, DK, Everybody's Hungary withdrew to back Tisza
EUobserver (March 30): Orbán's candidates losing in key constituencies
counter_evidence: Fidesz has gerrymandered electoral system advantage; could theoretically win fewer votes but more seats — but polling consistency and opposition consolidation make this scenario unlikely.
thesis: Viktor Orbán ends 16 years in power on April 12. Market overprices PM odds at 33.5% despite Tisza's 15-23pp polling lead. Opposition has consolidated support behind Péter Magyar's Tisza Party; even pro-government pollsters show Fidesz trailing. The only real counter-scenario is that the gerrymandered electoral system delivers Fidesz more seats despite fewer votes — a low-probability outcome given the scale of Tisza's lead and the timing proximity.
x_thread: Orbán's 16-year rule ends April 12. Market prices his PM odds at 33.5%. Tisza leads by 15-23pp in latest polls. We estimate 20% — market overpriced by 13.5pp.
Evidence: Tisza 49.6% (PolitPro), 56% among decided voters (21 Research). Opposition consolidated. Fidesz gerrymandered advantage exists but can't overcome this lead.
